removed: The system shall assign responsibility for managing the budget to individual teams, not solely to the IFA.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget.
reason: Both assign budget management responsibility to teams; [2] is less specific than [0].

removed: The system shall allow the IFA to specify budget policies.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the IFA to audit team budgets and set regulatory policies.
reason: Specifying budget policies is part of auditing and setting regulatory policies; [5] is redundant with [4].

removed: The system shall allow the aggregated data to be accessible to all users.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow all users to view the complete aggregated data.
reason: Allowing all users to view aggregated data is the same as making aggregated data accessible to all users.

removed: The system shall allow each team to access only their own data.
duplicate_of: The system shall restrict budget transaction entry to individual teams, with each team only able to access and modify their own data.
reason: Both restrict team data access to individual teams; [19] is less specific than [17].

removed: The system shall allow the insertion of budget transactions and items only by the respective teams.
duplicate_of: The system shall restrict budget transaction entry to individual teams, with each team only able to access and modify their own data.
reason: Allowing only teams to insert transactions implies restricted access, which is covered by [17].

removed: The system shall facilitate the definition of referee assignment policies and automatically assign referees to games based on those policies.
duplicate_of: The system shall have the ability to define policies for scheduling games.
reason: Defining referee assignment policies is a form of scheduling policy definition mentioned in [22].

removed: The system shall take into account user-specified availability constraints, such as pre-planned vacations, when scheduling games.
duplicate_of: The system shall enable referees to set their availability preferences.
reason: User-specified availability constraints are a type of availability preference set by referees.

removed: The system shall allow automatic scheduling and override by the IFA representative.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
reason: Automatic scheduling and override by IFA representative is a form of manual adjustment mentioned in [29].

removed: The system shall allow IFA representatives to perform both the initiator and approver roles in the scheduling process.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the IFA representative administration role to manage budgeting, scheduling, and all related activities.
reason: Performing initiator and approver roles in scheduling is part of managing scheduling activities covered in [64].

removed: The system shall send a notification to users when a message is posted by a player such as Messi.
duplicate_of: The system shall send push email notifications to fans when their favorite players post new messages.
reason: Sending notifications when a player posts a message is the same as in [33], with [35] being less general.

removed: The system shall send a notification to users who are registered for a game when a player replacement is reported by the referee after the game has started.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow fans to register for notifications about specific games and send them notifications when related updates occur.
reason: Sending notifications to registered users about game updates duplicates the functionality in [34].

removed: The system shall allow users to subscribe to each type of information mentioned and receive notifications accordingly.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow fans to register for notifications about specific games and send them notifications when related updates occur.
reason: Allowing subscription to information types and receiving notifications is the same as in [34].

removed: The system shall provide a web-based application for teams and IFA administrators to use.
duplicate_of: The system shall support multiple interfaces for different user roles or purposes.
reason: Providing a web-based application is a specific interface covered under supporting multiple interfaces in [41].

removed: The system shall provide mobile applications for referees and fans to interact with the system more easily.
duplicate_of: The system shall support multiple interfaces for different user roles or purposes.
reason: Providing mobile apps for referees and fans is part of supporting multiple interfaces in [41].

removed: The system shall provide a unified system with the same goal as the current separate systems.
duplicate_of: The system shall include all functionality within the application or website.
reason: Providing a unified system is related to including all functionality, but phrasing is vague; kept as low confidence.

removed: The system shall ensure that referees and official channels are the sole sources of game data within the system.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow only referees to update game data.
reason: Ensuring referees are the sole source of game data is equivalent to allowing only referees to update it.

removed: The system shall provide a response to the referee within a reasonable time frame.
duplicate_of: The system shall enable real-time communication between referees and the application during games.
reason: Providing a response within a reasonable time is implied by real-time communication in [48].

removed: The system shall be highly responsive and prioritize referee communications over other stadium activities.
duplicate_of: The system shall enable real-time communication between referees and the application during games.
reason: Prioritizing referee communications supports real-time communication mentioned in [48].

removed: The system shall enable the referee to receive responses immediately.
duplicate_of: The system shall enable real-time communication between referees and the application during games.
reason: Immediate responses to referees are part of real-time communication in [48].

removed: The system shall support a live recording feature, possibly including input from a side referee.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the referee to use a mobile phone to record game events in real time.
reason: Live recording with side referee input is a form of real-time game event recording by referees.

removed: The system shall comply with data residency restrictions imposed by certain countries.
duplicate_of: The system shall be adjustable to comply with local regulations and local review requirements.
reason: Complying with data residency restrictions is a specific case of complying with local regulations.

removed: The system shall support configuration of data center locations for labeling and allocation purposes.
duplicate_of: The system shall be adjustable to comply with local regulations and local review requirements.
reason: Configuring data center locations supports compliance with local regulations in [55].

removed: The system shall make all data accessible to everyone except for budget data, which shall be restricted.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the IFA to view the entire monetary information.
reason: Making data accessible to everyone except budget data is partially covered by IFA viewing all monetary info in [20], but access control context differs.

removed: The system shall allow the referee to update game information in real time.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the referee to insert game events in real time.
reason: Allowing referee to update game info in real time is the same as inserting game events in real time.

removed: The system shall allow referees to be appointed by the IFA.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow an IFA representative to invite a referee to join the system.
reason: Appointing a referee is equivalent to inviting them to join the system.

removed: The system shall enable referees to enter match events into the application in real time to ensure accountability and compliance auditing.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow referees to enter match events into the application in real time during the match.
reason: Entering match events in real time is the same functionality, phrased identically in both.

removed: The system shall automatically notify any fan who has registered to follow a specific game if the schedule for that game is changed.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow fans to register for notifications about specific games and send them notifications when related updates occur.
reason: Notifying fans of schedule changes is a specific case of registered game notifications in [34].
